title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize `(gep <not i8> p, (div exact X, C))`
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by recognizing that the original pattern—where a GEP index is an exact division/shift—can be extended beyond constant divisors to include variable divisors. The key insight is that when the index is an exact instruction (SDiv/UDiv/LShr) with a constant RHS, the element type scaling can be folded into the divisor/shift amount, converting the GEP to an i8* base. This transforms:

`gep T* X, (V / C)` → `gep i8* X, (V / (C * sizeof(T)))`

provided `C % sizeof(T) == 0` and the division remains exact.

The patch adds logic to handle all exact binary operators, checks for one-use to avoid increasing instruction count, and safely adjusts the constant. This makes the optimization applicable to any loop where the GEP index is an exact division by a constant multiple of the element size, not just the specific truncation pattern in the original test.
